trade.summary: Growing bilateral trade. Malaysia exports electronics, palm oil, rubber, and chemicals; Turkey exports steel, textiles, machinery, and chemicals. Both countries have actively promoted their economic relationship as Muslim-majority emerging markets with diversification interests.

diplomatic.summary: Strong friendly relations underpinned by shared Islamic identity and mutual interest in strengthening Muslim-majority country cooperation. Former Malaysian PM Mahathir and President Erdogan had particularly close personal ties and co-hosted the Kuala Lumpur Summit 2019 (an alternative Islamic forum). Both countries coordinate in OIC and D-8 (Developing 8) groupings. Turkey sees Malaysia as a key partner in Southeast Asia.

military.summary: Growing defense cooperation. Turkey has exported Kirpi armored vehicles and MILGEM-class corvettes to Malaysia. Malaysia and Turkey conduct bilateral military exchanges and have signed defense cooperation MOUs.

financial.summary: Growing investment flows. Turkish companies are expanding in Malaysia; Malaysian Islamic finance institutions cooperate with Turkish participation sukuk markets. Malaysian palm oil companies export to Turkey as a significant consumer.

energy.summary: Malaysia exports palm oil-based biofuels and some petroleum products to Turkey. Turkey is a significant importer of Malaysian palm oil. No major oil or gas bilateral flows.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific exchanges in Islamic economics, halal standards, and technology. Turkish and Malaysian universities cooperate through OIC scientific frameworks.
